paragraphs.body: Wrestling is more than just a battle on the mat, it is a powerful teacher that shapes character and instills resilience. You might be asking yourself why is he writing an essay about wrestling? Well for me, wrestling has shaped me into the man I am today, and I am confident it has led me here!

paragraphs.body: To my family, wrestling holds much more meaning than just a sport. It is a Legacy! It is woven into my family history and marked by dedication and toughness. My father and uncle not only embraced this demanding discipline, but they also excelled at it. They created a legacy that inspires me and shapes our family’s identity. The stories that they share with me not only are testaments to hard work and dedication, but they demonstrate how passion can transform lives and create champions. My uncle, Clint Walbeck was a California state champion, and he wrestled at Cal Poly University for 2 years before transferring to Fresno State to finish his college career. His achievements were not just accolades, but they reflected his relentless dedication to the sport. My father, Grant Enzo, also had an apparent love and passion for wrestling. He was a two-sport athlete at Fresno State University, where he wrestled and played football. Their bond over the sport deepened as they trained together and pushed each other to be the best they could be. They instilled in me their belief system that hard work and determination can lead to success, whether in athletics or other pursuits.

annotations.comment: Great details throughout this paragraph! It is a cool legacy that you share :)

annotations.context_left: ystem that hard work and determination can lead to success, whether in athletics or other pursuits.

paragraphs.body: Recently I read an article by Kase Nipe titled “How Wrestling Prepares You for Life.” In this article he demonstrates the mindset that wrestlers have and how physically demanding the sport can be. One thing Kase wrote that really stuck with me were these wise words, “Wrestling is one of the most difficult and demanding sports in all of athletics. Not only does it require physical strength, agility, and stamina, but also an impressive discernment for strategy and tactics that can give a wrestler an advantage over his or her opponents.” (Nipe). This quote describes the complexity of wrestling, which is why I find it appealing. It is a mental game just as much as a physical one, and it is not just about physical stamina or strength. In each wrestling match, the wrestler has unique styles and tactics, so there is no preparation. During a match you are required to assess those differences and adjust your strategies spontaneously. This aspect of wrestling mirrors real-life situations where the ability to think critically and remain composed under pressure can lead to long-lasting success.

annotations.text: This aspect of wrestling mirrors real-life situations where the ability to think critically and remain composed under pressure can lead to long-lasting success.

paragraphs.body: During my senior year in high school wrestling season, I was simply training and living life, until one day after practice I got a call from my mom saying my grandfather was in the hospital and not expected to live much longer. My grandfather, Mark Walbeck, had stage 4 liver cancer and once he tested positive for covid, his body was not strong enough to fight off either infection. His death was a shock to me because he had raised me throughout the process of my mom and dad’s divorce. He taught me everything I know today and upon hearing that diagnosis it devastated me. My grandpa Mark was a very pivotal figure in my life. His influence modeled how I approach challenges and adversity still to this day.

annotations.comment: This must have been such a difficult experience for you. Thanks for sharing it.
annotations.context_left: figure in my life. His influence modeled how I approach challenges and adversity still to this day.

paragraphs.body: Losing him left a void in my heart, but also sparked a flame in me. After his death I was determined to honor him by living like a champion. He was my biggest inspiration and my biggest supporter; he was always there for me! In the wake of his passing, I found myself reflecting on the lessons he taught me throughout the years. I realized that champions are defined not only by their victories but also by how they rise after setbacks. Inspired by this, I look at every challenge on, or off the mat, as an opportunity to grow and build my character. I discovered how to turn my sorrow into inspiration and use it to work harder in my training and further pursue greatness. Every step onto the mat is a tribute to him, every match is a chance to embody the values he taught me, and every pin I hear his voice calling me to always carry onward the legacy he gifted to my family.

annotations.text: realized that champions are defined not only by their victories but also by how they rise after setbacks.

paragraphs.body: By living with purpose and resilience, I remind myself that real champions overcome hardship and move forward with determination, I make it my mission to hold true to these mottos and make him proud. That is why I am so confident that wrestling is the reason that I am here. When times are tough in a match, you might be down, time is running out, and your brain tends to not try as hard because you already know the outcome... but if you can push past that point, that's where champions are made! That is how I have lived my life since his passing because I want to be a NCAA champion and make my grandpa proud in heaven. I just imagine how proud he already is of me for making it to college on a scholarship and becoming number 3 in all Cailfornia! Wrestling is not just about physical strength; it is a transformative experience that teaches you about the power of your mind. It dares you to confront your limits, develop resilience, and embrace discipline. You will learn to strategize, adapt under pressure, and appreciate the value of challenging work. More than just a sport, wrestling cultivates life skills that can profoundly impact your personal and professional journey.

annotations.text: When times are tough in a match, you might be down, time is running out, and your brain tends to not try as hard because you already know the outcome... but if you can push past that point, that's where champions are made!
annotations.comment: Nice--I wonder if this idea holds true off the mat as well? Should you make some connections?

paragraphs.body: Wrestling is a powerful journey that goes far beyond physical competition. By stepping onto the mat, individuals can unlock priceless life lessons, such as resilience, discipline, and strategic thinking. For example, my grandfather’s passing was a large hurdle for me, but instead of allowing my grief to end my senior season early, I used it as fuel to get me to the next level. I knew he would not want me to sit out and be devastated over his death, but instead remember his lessons and apply them in every aspect of my life, especially wrestling. Today, all of us can learn a thing or two from the great Mark Walbeck; embrace the challenge in front of you and live life like a champion!!!
